exemplar.title: To what extent do you agree with the claim "all models are wrong, but some are useful" (attributed to George Box)? Discuss with reference to mathematics and one other area of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent do you agree with the claim "all models are wrong, but some are useful" (attributed to George Box)? Discuss with reference to mathematics and one other area of knowledge.

strengths: Sustained and focused engagement with the prescribed title, using Boxâs quotation as an organising thread throughout the essay
Clear, coherent arguments that are logically sequenced and effectively linked to both mathematics and the human sciences
Use of pertinent, real-world examples (e.g., SIR model, costâbenefit analysis, population-growth projections, diagnostic categories) to illustrate both the usefulness and limitations of models
Awareness of different points of view, with explicit acknowledgement of opposing perspectives (useful vs. harmful models, simplicity vs. oversimplification)
weaknesses: Evaluation of counter-perspectives remains relatively general, without deep interrogation of underlying assumptions in each area of knowledge
Some examples are analysed only descriptively, and occasional conflation of methodologies (e.g., geography vs. human sciences) limits nuance
Conclusion largely restates earlier points without synthesising broader implications for knowledge production or suggesting further lines of inquiry
Stylistic and structural issues, such as repetitive phrasing and uneven signposting, hinder the depth and fluidity of the argument
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The official examinerâs total of 8 places this essay at the upper end of the 7â8 band. The student sustains a clear focus on the title, develops coherent and logically sequenced arguments, and supports claims with relevant, real-world examples across two areas of knowledge. There is awareness and some evaluation of alternative viewpoints. However, the depth of critical interrogation and the sophistication of evaluation do not reach the sustained engagement characteristic of the 9â10 band. On balance, the essay exemplifies a strong performance meriting a score of 8, reflecting its clear strengths alongside identifiable areas for deeper analytical development.

annotations.comment: The opening analogy presenting the universe as an intricate system of models effectively engages the reader and establishes a clear context for discussing simplification and inaccuracy.
The introduction lacks an explicit thesis statement stating the studentâs precise stance on whether model limitations outweigh usefulness; a clear claim would guide the subsequent analysis.
The summary sentence emphasising the need for accuracy and unbiasedness in human-sciences models succinctly reinforces the essayâs central focus.
The population-growth model example is well-chosen to show practical application of simplified models in resource planning, reinforcing the usefulness claim.
The claim that axioms âleave no space for ambiguityâ overlooks debates about foundational assumptions in mathematics; acknowledging contested axioms would add nuance.
The costâbenefit analysis example is applied effectively to illustrate how mathematical models aid business decision-making, grounding abstract discussion in real-world context.
This example description omits critical evaluation of assumptions such as chosen discount rates and omission of qualitative factors, limiting the depth of analysis.
The use of the SIR model as a case study for pandemic policy demonstrates effective linkage between mathematical modelling and public-health decision-making.
The SIR discussion omits common assumptions such as homogeneous mixing and fixed transmission rates; acknowledging these would clarify the modelâs limitations.
The historical example of hysteria diagnoses effectively illustrates how cultural biases can distort human-sciences models and lead to harmful treatments.
Analysis of the hysteria case stops short of exploring its implications for contemporary diagnostic frameworks; deeper reflection on current methodologies is needed.
Conflating geography with the human sciences neglects methodological differences; distinguishing qualitative versus quantitative approaches would clarify the discussion.
Relying solely on a Pew Research report limits academic depth; incorporating peer-reviewed demographic studies would strengthen the evidence base.
The explanation of kinematic model variables (force, angle, time) demonstrates clear technical understanding of mathematical modelling.
The discussion would benefit from inclusion of the formal equation $$s = ut + \tfrac{1}{2}at^2$$ to illustrate how simplifications affect quantitative predictions.
Paragraph openings often repeat phrases like âIt can be arguedâ or âOverallâ; varying academic phrasing would strengthen style.
The balance of breadth over depth means some examples receive only descriptive treatment; focusing on fewer cases for deeper critique would enhance evaluation.
Comparing the effects of air resistance and gravitational variation shows a nuanced evaluation of which factors meaningfully impact model accuracy.
Introducing a contrasting case where oversimplification succeeded or failed would deepen the analysis of when to stop reducing model complexity.
The conclusion largely restates prior points without synthesising broader implications for knowledge production or suggesting areas for further inquiry.
Reaffirming that models remain useful when reliable and unbiased effectively ties the conclusion back to the prescribed title quotation.
Discussion of âusefulnessâ focuses on practical applicability; reflecting on how usefulness influences trust in scientific claims would deepen the TOK analysis.
The Works Cited demonstrates consistent application of citation conventions and a range of sources across disciplines.
Several entries omit page numbers or DOIs; ensuring complete bibliographic details would improve academic rigor.
Essay structure is logical but could benefit from clearer signposting of counterarguments and smoother transitions between AOKs.
Greater engagement with implications of model limitations for knowledge authorityâfor instance, how oversimplification affects public trustâwould deepen the TOK focus.
Several examples lack explicit tie-back to Boxâs quotation; ensure each case analysis clearly references how models are âwrong yet useful.â

The essay does not
reach a standard
described by levels
1â5 or is not a
response to one of
the prescribed titles
on the list for the
current session.
The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
